session.user:
Search the codebase for remaining references to "reason" in the context of RoadmapNode or roadmap operations. Specifically:
1. Search for `reason` in all Python files under `src/` and `packages/` and `tests/` that might be related to RoadmapNode or roadmap operations 2. Look for patterns like `reason=`, `.reason`, `"reason"`, `--reason` in files related to objectives/roadmaps 3. Exclude files I've already updated: - packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py - packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/dependency_graph.py - src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_objective_node.py - src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/add_objective_node.py - src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_render_roadmap.py - tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_update_objective_node.py - tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_add_objective_node.py
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
Look especially in test files for roadmap parsing, any other exec scripts, and any shared module tests.

session.tool_result:
1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)

session.assistant: Good! There's documentation on roadmap topics. Let me read the relevant roadmap documentation files.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Roadmap Format Versioning 3→read_when: 4→ - extending the roadmap table format with new columns 5→ - planning backward-compatible parser changes to roadmap tables 6→ - understanding roadmap column format history 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "adding columns to the roadmap table format" 9→ warning: "Read this doc to understand the header-based detection and rendering strategy before adding columns." 10→ - action: "referencing a 'plan' field on RoadmapNode" 11→ warning: "The plan field was removed (PR #8128). RoadmapNode has: id, description, status, pr, depends_on, slug. Plan references are no longer tracked in the roadmap." 12→last_audited: "2026-02-26 00:00 PT" 13→audit_result: edited 14→--- 15→ 16→# Roadmap Format Versioning 17→ 18→This document captures the design thinking around the roadmap table format, including the current 4-column canonical format and historical context. 19→ 20→## Current State: 4-Column Format (Canonical) 21→ 22→<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py, RoadmapNode --> 23→ 24→The `RoadmapNode` dataclass has six fields (`id`, `description`, `status`, `pr`, `depends_on`, `slug`). The `pr` field holds a PR reference (`"#123"`) for both in-progress and landed PRs and is `str | None`. The `depends_on` field holds explicit dependencies (`tuple[str, ...] | None`), and `slug` holds a kebab-case identifier (`str | None`). The `plan` field was removed in PR #8128 — plan references are no longer tracked in the roadmap table. 25→ 26→The canonical rendered table format: 27→ 28→```markdown 29→| Node | Description | Status | PR | 30→| ---- | ----------- | ----------- | ---- |
... [50 lines omitted] ...
46→Previous formats are no longer actively parsed by `parse_roadmap()`. The parser requires v2+ YAML frontmatter and returns a legacy format error for non-v2+ content. Historical formats included a separate Plan column and a combined PR column with `plan #N` syntax. 59→- **v2/v3/v4 parsing**: `parse_roadmap()` accepts all three schema versions. Returns a legacy format error for non-v2/v3/v4 content (no table-parsing fallback)

1→--- 2→title: Roadmap Mutation Patterns 3→read_when: 4→ - "deciding between surgical vs full-body roadmap updates" 5→ - "choosing how to update an objective roadmap after a workflow event" 6→ - "understanding race condition risks in roadmap table mutations" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "using full-body update for single-cell changes" 9→ warning: "Full-body updates replace the entire table. For single-node PR updates, use surgical update (update-objective-node) to preserve other cells and avoid race conditions." 10→ - action: "using surgical update for complete table rewrites" 11→ warning: "Surgical updates only change one node. For rewriting roadmaps after landing PRs (status + layout changes), use full-body update (system:objective-update-with-landed-pr)." 12→ - action: "directly mutating issue body markdown without using either command" 13→ warning: "Direct body mutation skips status computation. The surgical command writes computed status atomically; bypassing it leaves status stale. See roadmap-mutation-semantics.md." 14→ - action: "using None/empty string interchangeably in update-objective-node parameters" 15→ warning: "None=preserve existing value, empty string=clear the cell, value=set new value. Confusing these leads to accidental data loss or stale values." 16→last_audited: "2026-02-25 18:00 PT" 17→audit_result: edited 18→--- 19→ 20→# Roadmap Mutation Patterns 21→ 22→Erk has two distinct strategies for mutating objective roadmap tables, each optimized for different workflow events. Choosing the wrong one creates race conditions or data loss. This document explains **when to use which** and **why the split exists**. 23→ 24→## Why Two Patterns? 25→ 26→The roadmap table is a 4-column markdown table stored in a GitHub issue comment (`| Node | Description | Status | PR |`). The YAML frontmatter in the issue body is the source of truth; the comment table is a rendered view. Two fundamentally different mutation shapes arise from different workflow events: 27→ 28→- **Single-node updates** (plan saved, PR created): Only the status/PR of one step changes. The YAML frontmatter is updated surgically, then the comment table is deterministically re-rendered from YAML. 29→ 30→- **Full-body rewrites** (PR landed): Landing a PR may trigger structural changes — marking multiple steps done, updating descriptions, or adding narrative text. The `objective-apply-landed-update` command handles batch updates.
... [88 lines omitted] ...
112→- [Discriminated Union Error Handling](../architecture/discriminated-union-error-handling.md) — LBYL error patterns used by both commands

1→--- 2→title: Roadmap Parser 3→last_audited: "2026-02-17 16:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "understanding how roadmap nodes are parsed" 7→ - "working with objective roadmap check or update commands" 8→ - "debugging roadmap parsing issues" 9→ - "using erk objective check or erk exec update-objective-node" 10→tripwires: 11→ - action: "implementing roadmap parsing functionality" 12→ warning: "The parser is regex-based, not LLM-based. Do not reference LLM inference." 13→ - action: "creating or modifying roadmap step IDs" 14→ warning: "Step IDs should use plain numbers (1.1, 2.1), not letter format (1A.1, 1B.1)." 15→--- 16→ 17→# Roadmap Parser 18→ 19→This document describes how erk parses and mutates objective roadmap tables using regex-based parsing. 20→ 21→## Overview 22→ 23→Erk uses deterministic regex parsing to extract structured data from objective roadmap markdown tables. Two commands expose this functionality: 24→ 25→- **`erk objective check`** — parse and validate a roadmap, returning human-readable or structured JSON output 26→- **`erk exec update-objective-node`** — surgically update a specific step's PR column 27→ 28→Both commands share the parser in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py`. 29→ 30→## Check Command
... [104 lines omitted] ...
49→The parser emits warnings (not errors) for: 106→The parser accepts both formats but emits a validation warning for letter-format IDs. All templates now use plain numbers.

session.assistant: Those "reason" references are not related to RoadmapNode - they're for tripwire candidates and status history. Let me now check the specific exec scripts related to roadmap operations:
session.tool_result:
1→"""Update node PR cells in an objective's roadmap table. 2→ 3→Why this command exists (instead of using update-issue-body directly): 4→ 5→ The alternative is "fetch body -> parse markdown table -> find node row -> 6→ surgically edit the PR cell -> write entire body back". That's ~15 7→ lines of fragile ad-hoc Python that every caller (skills, hooks, scripts) 8→ must duplicate. The roadmap table has a specific structure 9→ (| node_id | description | status | pr |) and the update has 10→ specific semantics: 11→ 12→ 1. Find the row by node ID across all phases 13→ 2. Compute display status from the PR value 14→ 3. Write status and PR cells atomically so the table is 15→ always human-readable without requiring a parse pass 16→ 17→ Encoding this once in a tested CLI command means: 18→ - No duplicated table-parsing logic across callers 19→ - Testable edge cases (node not found, no roadmap, clearing PR) 20→ - Atomic mental model: "update node 1.3's PR to X" 21→ - Resilient to roadmap format changes (one command updates, not N sites) 22→ 23→Usage: 24→ # Single node -- landed PR 25→ erk exec update-objective-node 6423 --node 1.3 --pr "#6500" --status done 26→ 27→ # Clear PR 28→ erk exec update-objective-node 6423 --node 1.3 --pr "" 29→ 30→ # Status-only update (preserve existing PR)
... [529 lines omitted] ...
41→ Each node result: {node_id, success, previous_pr, error} 80→def _node_error_message(node_id: str, issue_number: int, error: object) -> str: 81→ if error == "node_not_found": 83→ return f"Failed to replace cells for node '{node_id}'" 94→ backlink_warning: str | None 99→ return cls(backlink_set=True, backlink_warning=None, backlink_skip=None) 102→ def warning(cls, *, message: str) -> BacklinkResult: 103→ return cls(backlink_set=False, backlink_warning=message, backlink_skip=None) 107→ return cls(backlink_set=False, backlink_warning=None, backlink_skip=reason) 115→ error: str | None 119→ return cls(node_id=node_id, success=True, previous_pr=previous_pr, error=None) 122→ def fail(cls, *, node_id: str, error: str) -> UpdateObjectiveNodeResult: 123→ return cls(node_id=node_id, success=False, previous_pr=None, error=error) 127→class UpdateObjectiveNodeError: 128→ error: str 132→ return {"success": False, "error": self.error, "message": self.message} 187→ UpdateObjectiveNodeMultiOutput | UpdateObjectiveNodeError | UpdateObjectiveNodeSuccess 219→ return UpdateObjectiveNodeError( 220→ error=single.error or "unknown", 221→ message=_node_error_message(node[0], issue_number, single.error), 305→ except ValueError: 322→ Never raises — backlink failure doesn't block the node update. 331→ return BacklinkResult.warning(message=f"PR {pr_ref} not found") 339→ return BacklinkResult.warning( 422→ err = UpdateObjectiveNodeError( 423→ error="no_update", 436→ err = UpdateObjectiveNodeError( 437→ error="issue_not_found", 446→ err = UpdateObjectiveNodeError( 447→ error="no_roadmap", 458→ UpdateObjectiveNodeResult.fail(node_id=n, error="node_not_found") for n in missing_nodes 476→ any_failure = False 481→ results.append(UpdateObjectiveNodeResult.fail(node_id=node_id, error="node_not_found")) 482→ any_failure = True 496→ UpdateObjectiveNodeResult.fail(node_id=node_id, error="replacement_failed") 498→ any_failure = True 504→ # Exit early if all nodes failed 505→ if any_failure and not any(r.success for r in results): 534→ # failure doesn't prevent the node update from succeeding.

1→"""Apply mechanical updates to an objective after landing a PR. 2→ 3→Combines the fetch-context, update-nodes, and post-action-comment steps into 4→a single call, eliminating 5+ sequential agent commands. Only prose 5→reconciliation (which requires LLM judgment) is left to the calling skill. 6→ 7→The PR number doubles as the plan number (in erk, the PR IS the plan). 8→ 9→Usage: 10→ erk exec objective-apply-landed-update --pr 6517 --objective 6423 --branch P6517-... 11→ erk exec objective-apply-landed-update # auto-discovers all arguments 12→ 13→Output: 14→ JSON with {success, objective, plan, pr, roadmap, node_updates, 15→ action_comment_id} or {success, error} 16→ 17→Exit Codes: 18→ 0: Success - all mechanical updates applied 19→ 1: Error - missing data or API failure 20→""" 21→ 22→import json 23→ 24→import click 25→ 26→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.objective_fetch_context import ( 27→ _build_roadmap_context, 28→ _fetch_objective_content, 29→) 30→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.objective_post_action_comment import (
... [318 lines omitted] ...
57→ ApplyLandedUpdateErrorDict, 69→def _error_json(error: str) -> str: 70→ result: ApplyLandedUpdateErrorDict = {"success": False, "error": error} 194→ click.echo(_error_json("Cannot determine owner/repo from current context")) 206→ click.echo(_error_json("Could not determine current branch (detached HEAD?)")) 213→ click.echo(_error_json(f"No PR found for branch '{branch_name}'")) 220→ click.echo(_error_json(f"PR #{pr_number} not found")) 229→ click.echo(_error_json(msg)) 236→ click.echo(_error_json(f"Objective #{objective_number} not found")) 242→ click.echo(_error_json(f"PR #{pr_number} not found"))

1→"""Add a new node to an objective's roadmap. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec add-objective-node 8470 \ 5→ --phase 1 \ 6→ --description "Clean up dead modify_existing code" \ 7→ [--slug cleanup-modify-existing] \ 8→ [--status pending] \ 9→ [--depends-on 1.2] [--depends-on 1.3] \ 10→ [--comment "Added during objective re-evaluation"] 11→ 12→Output: 13→ JSON with {success, issue_number, node_id, url} 14→ 15→Exit Codes: 16→ 0: Always. 1→"""Shared parser, serializer, and data types for objective roadmap operations. 2→ 3→This module provides: 4→- Data types: RoadmapNodeStatus, RoadmapNode, RoadmapPhase 5→- Parsing: parse_roadmap() (v2 frontmatter only) 6→- Frontmatter: validate, parse, group, update 7→- Utilities: compute_summary(), find_next_node(), serialize_phases() 8→ 9→Previously split across objective_roadmap_shared.py and 10→objective_roadmap_frontmatter.py in the erk package. Consolidated 11→here to eliminate the circular dependency between erk_shared and erk. 12→""" 13→ 14→import re 15→from collections.abc import Mapping 16→from dataclasses import dataclass, replace 17→from typing import Literal, cast 18→ 19→import yaml 20→ 21→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import ( 22→ extract_raw_metadata_blocks, 23→ parse_metadata_block_body, 24→) 25→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.types import BlockKeys 26→ 27→RoadmapNodeStatus = Literal["pending", "planning", "done", "in_progress", "blocked", "skipped"] 28→ 29→ 30→@dataclass(frozen=True)

session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive final report:
## Summary: Remaining References to "reason" in RoadmapNode Context
I've completed a thorough search of the codebase for remaining references to the old "reason" field in RoadmapNode-related code. Here are my findings:
### Key Finding: The Rename is Incomplete
The `reason` field was renamed to `comment` in the RoadmapNode dataclass, but **4 files still use the old parameter name** when calling functions that expect the new `comment` parameter.
### Files With Remaining Issues
All issues are parameter name mismatches where `reason=None` is being passed but the function signature expects `comment`:
1. **src/erk/cli/commands/objective/plan_cmd.py** (3 occurrences) - Line 393: `reason=None,` in `_replace_node_refs_in_body()` call - Line 447: `reason=None,` in `_replace_node_refs_in_body()` call - Line 499: `reason=None,` in `_replace_node_refs_in_body()` call
2. **src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_apply_landed_update.py** (1 occurrence) - Line 99: `reason=None,` in `_replace_node_refs_in_body()` call
3. **src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_link_pr.py** (1 occurrence) - Line 117: `reason=None,` in `update_node_in_frontmatter()` call
### Function Signatures (Already Updated)
The function signatures in both cases have been correctly updated to use `comment`:
- **`_replace_node_refs_in_body()`** in `/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_objective_node.py` - Line 248-256: Parameters include `comment: str | None` (not `reason`)
- **`update_node_in_frontmatter()`** in `/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py` - Line 322-330: Parameters include `comment: str | None` (not `reason`)
### RoadmapNode Dataclass (Already Updated)
The dataclass itself is already correct: - `/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py`, lines 30-40 - Field 40: `comment: str | None` (no `reason` field)
### Test Coverage
The test file `/tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_update_objective_node.py` is already using the correct parameter name `comment=None` in all test calls, so tests are aligned with the new signature.
### Files Already Updated (Not Included in This Search)
According to the exclusion list you provided, these files have already been updated: - packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py - packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/dependency_graph.py - src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_objective_node.py - src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/add_objective_node.py - src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_render_roadmap.py - tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_update_objective_node.py - tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_add_objective_node.py
### Absolute File Paths Needing Updates
1. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/objective/plan_cmd.py` (lines 393, 447, 499) 2. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_apply_landed_update.py` (line 99) 3. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_link_pr.py` (line 117)
